items.title: Baker Tilly Eyes $3 Billion in Debt to Replace Private Credit
items.description: Deutsche Bank AG is preparing to sell about $3 billion in debt markets for accounting services firm Baker Tilly Advisory Group to refinance private credit loans, according to people familiar with the

items.title: Gauge of Oracle’s Credit Risk Hits Fresh All Time High
items.description: A measure of Oracle Corp.’s credit risk hit a record high on Friday as concerns grew about both the company’s aggressive cash-burn and the future profitability of its artificial intelligence investmen

items.title: AI Upheaval Crushes Retail Traders Crowding Into Leveraged Funds
items.description: A surprise breakthrough from Chinese startup Moonshot rippled through global markets Friday, sending AI and semiconductor stocks sharply lower and reviving memories of last year’s so-called DeepSeek m

items.title: Martin Shkreli Is Back, Shorting Stocks and Taking On Everyone
items.description: Martin Shkreli wants to make one thing clear to anyone who’s willing to listen: He’s back and he still doesn’t care how many people he ticks off along the way.

items.title: Hedge Funds Add Bullish Oil Bets at Fastest Pace in a Decade
items.description: Hedge funds piled into bullish bets on Brent oil at the fastest pace in almost a decade as fighting between Iran and the US ramped up, dampening investors’ expectations of a sustained recovery in ship

items.title: Argentina Oil-Boom Province to Pitch First US Bond Since 2017
items.description: Argentina’s Neuquen province will pitch foreign investors in New York on its first new international bond in nearly a decade as the local government reaps the rewards of a shale oil boom, according to

items.title: Treasuries Post Weekly Advance as Inflation Optimism Prevails
items.description: The Treasury market eked out a gain this week as supportive inflation data prevailed over a rebound in oil prices, leading traders to mostly abandon bets on a July Federal Reserve interest-rate hike.

items.title: US Oil Rig Count Notches Longest Streak Without Drop Since 2022
items.description: Drilling in the US oil patch achieved the longest stretch without a decrease in activity in more than four years, supporting shale’s expansion amid higher prices driven by the Iran war.

items.title: Blue Owl-Backed Latigo Files for IPO, Adding to Biotech Pipeline
items.description: Latigo Biotherapeutics Inc. filed for an initial public offering, joining a growing list of biotechnology firms considering going public as soon as this quarter.

items.title: Sweetgreen Pares Drop as Parasite Tied to Taco Bell Lettuce
items.description: Sweetgreen Inc. shares surged on Friday, halting a four-day skid, after US regulators said a parasite outbreak that’s sickened thousands across several states was traced to shredded iceberg lettuce se

items.title: Griffin Wants to Buy Old Cottage Across From New Citadel HQ
items.description: Ken Griffin has gone to extraordinary lengths to assemble parcels of land around his planned Citadel headquarters in Miami, including buying up all 138 units in a condo building across the street.

items.title: Hedge Fund Blasts SEC Settlement After ‘Gag Rule’ Lifted
items.description: Connecticut hedge fund Deccan Value Investors is speaking out about its 2022 settlement of Securities and Exchange Commission claims that it breached its fiduciary duty to two university endowments af

items.title: Apple Briefly Tops Nvidia as World’s Most Valuable Company
items.description: Apple Inc. briefly overtook Nvidia Corp. as the world’s most valuable company, a title the chipmaker has held since May 2025.

items.title: Chips Stocks Sink Into Bear Market as 105% AI Rally Fizzles
items.description: A closely watched index of semiconductor stocks fell into a bear market, unwinding a chunk of the blistering memory-led rally that saw the gauge more than double in just three months.

items.title: US Stocks End Week Lower on Fears of Another ‘DeepSeek Moment’
items.description: US stocks finished the week lower as semiconductor names dipped following the reveal of an artificial-intelligence model from a Chinese startup.

items.title: ‘Sea of Red’ Hits Emerging Stocks as AI Concerns Jolts Markets
items.description: Emerging-market stocks slumped, with a key gauge sliding into technical correction on mounting concerns the outlook for artificial intelligence no longer justifies the industry’s elevated valuations.

items.title: Gold Trims Weekly Drop on Dip-Buying Despite Rate Hike Odds
items.description: Gold trimmed its weekly loss as some investors bought on dips after the precious metal broke below a key price level, even as escalating war tensions kept interest rate hike bets alive.

items.title: Chip Stocks Slide Into Bear Market in AI Unwind: Markets Wrap
items.description: A selloff in chipmakers gathered pace, driving the high-profile group of stocks to a bear market on worries that the artificial-intelligence spending spree is becoming harder to justify.

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 17
items.description: Oil surged on growing fears the US and Iran could return to full war, threatening flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: CoreLogic Wraps Up $5.25 Billion Debt Deal With Hefty Yields
items.description: CoreLogic Inc. raised $5.25 billion from a debt sale after amending the terms and offering some of the year’s highest yields in response to lukewarm investor demand.
